_provenance.how: The SHAPE of each payload below was copied from actual `mcp__minsky__session_pr_merge` tool returns (the merges of PR #2227 and PR #2232). Only the shape is the fixture; see `_sanitization`.
_provenance.why: These fixtures exist because the previous ones did NOT. mt#3066's verification script authored its own `ToolHookInput` with `tool_result.session.sessionId` — a shape invented to match the resolver rather than captured from a real call. It passed while production skipped every merge. A hand-written fixture tests the reader against itself; only a captured one tests the reader against reality.
_provenance._sanitization: IDs are placeholders and the absolute session-directory path was removed (PR #2246 R1). The resolver keys off STRUCTURE — which key holds the id, at which nesting depth, as which type — so substituting the id values costs the fixture nothing and keeps a developer's home path and real conversation ids out of VCS. Do NOT restore real values when re-capturing.
_provenance.recapture: Call `session_pr_merge`, copy its return's STRUCTURE into `toolResult`, replace ids with placeholders of the same shape, drop any absolute path, and record the `tool_input` form you passed. If the structure differs from what is here, that is a real envelope change — update the resolver's accessor list, not just the fixture.

taskInvoked._description: The COMMON form: merge invoked with `task:` and no `sessionId`. This is the shape that never resolved — the id is a string at tool_result.result.session, one level deeper than the old resolver looked.

sessionIdInvoked._description: The same tool invoked with an explicit `sessionId`. This form always resolved, via tool_input — which is why the defect was invisible whenever a merge happened to be called this way.
